title: Software Stocks Are Sinking In 2026, But These 'Fab Five' Are Up 30% — And BofA Says They're Just Getting Started
author: Piero Cingari
description: Bank of America identifies five enterprise software stocks—Datadog, JFrog, MongoDB, Snowflake, and Twilio—as outperformers in 2026, collectively up 30% while the broader software sector declines 12%. The analyst attributes their strength to solid execution, AI exposure, and differentiated products, with AI-related revenue growing over 100% at Datadog and strong adoption across all five companies. BofA maintains Buy ratings on all five, expecting the gap between these winners and struggling peers to widen.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Surged 58% post-earnings with 32% YoY revenue growth acceleration. AI business growing over 100% annually and representing over 10% of revenue. Maintained Buy rating by BofA analyst.
Advanced 43% post-earnings with cloud revenue growth reaching 50%, up from 42% prior quarter. Increasing adoption among AI-focused customers. Maintained Buy rating.
Rose 5% post-earnings with Atlas revenue growth at 29.4%. Reported increasing adoption among AI-focused customers. BofA remains confident in long-term outlook with Buy rating maintained.
Climbed 37% post-earnings with product revenue growth accelerating to 34% YoY. AI products (Cortex AI and Cortex Code) driving growth. Maintained Buy rating.
Gained 38% post-earnings with gross-profit-dollar growth improving to 16% from 10%. Momentum from voice AI products. Maintained Buy rating.
Down 12% in 2026, representing the broader software sector decline that contrasts sharply with the outperformance of the 'Fab Five' stocks.

title: SalesCloser Deepens Conversational AI Moat with Dedicated GPU Inference Cluster, Enabling Custom Model Fine-Tuning, Agentic Workflows, and Regulated-Industry Readiness
author: Salescloser Technologies Ltd.
description: SalesCloser has commissioned a dedicated AI inference cluster using NVIDIA Blackwell-class GPUs hosted on Canadian renewable energy infrastructure. The owned inference layer enables custom model fine-tuning, sophisticated agentic workflows, data sovereignty for regulated industries, reduced vendor dependency, and sustainable operations—positioning the company for enterprise market expansion.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: SalesCloser's deployment of NVIDIA Blackwell-class GPUs for production AI inference represents validation of NVIDIA's enterprise AI infrastructure capabilities and demonstrates real-world adoption in conversational AI applications.

Mentioned only as a collaboration partner for phone number activation and compliance support. While the partnership indicates business development activity, the article provides insufficient detail about the scope or impact of the collaboration to warrant a stronger sentiment rating.

title: SalesCloser Announces Collaboration with Twilio to Accelerate Customer Deployments
author: Salescloser Technologies Ltd.
description: SalesCloser Technologies has entered into a collaboration with Twilio to simplify enterprise phone number activation and reduce deployment friction for its AI-powered sales agents. The partnership streamlines account setup, phone number provisioning, identity verification, and regulatory compliance, enabling faster time-to-launch and improved call reliability for SalesCloser customers.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Twilio is being positioned as a key infrastructure partner in a high-growth AI sales automation market, expanding its customer base through a streamlined referral and onboarding process with an emerging technology leader.

title: Nokia, Intel, And Sandisk Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (April 27-May 1): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks delivered strong performance during the week of April 27-May 1, driven by earnings beats and strategic announcements. Notable gainers include Atlassian (28.76%), Centene (25.68%), Twilio (27.36%), and Nokia (24.3%), with gains fueled by better-than-expected quarterly results, raised guidance, and analyst upgrades. Other top performers include Aurora Innovation, Bloom Energy, Seagate, NXP Semiconductors, Intel, and Sandisk.

Stock gained 25.68% after reporting better-than-expected Q1 results with multiple analyst price forecast increases

title: Why Twilio Stock Soared Today
author: Joe Tenebruso
description: Twilio stock surged 23.46% after the cloud communications company reported strong Q1 2026 results with 20% year-over-year revenue growth to $1.4 billion and 31% jump in adjusted operating income. The company raised its full-year guidance, citing unprecedented demand for its conversational AI voice technology. CEO Khozema Shipchandler highlighted AI as a foundational infrastructure layer driving growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q1 results with 20% YoY revenue growth, 31% increase in adjusted operating income, improved profitability metrics, and raised full-year guidance (14-15% revenue growth vs. prior 11.5-12.5%). Surging demand for AI-powered voice solutions positions the company well for continued growth.
